description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white and dark blue, featuring the Lufthansa livery and logo on the tail fin. The aircraft has two large turbofan engines mounted under its low-set, swept-back wings, which are equipped with small upward-angled winglets at the tips. The tail section consists of a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all in dark blue with the airline's emblem. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The landing gear is in a tricycle configuration, with two main gear units under the wings and a nose gear under the cockpit. The overall design is typical of modern short- to medium-haul commercial jets, optimized for passenger transport.
description: A Lufthansa-branded commercial jet with low, swept-back wings, twin underwing turbofan engines, and a conventional tail. The fuselage is white with a dark blue tail and winglets, and the cockpit is forward-mounted with multiple windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, Lufthansa branding, and the dark blue tail with logo.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, both engines, and the symmetry of the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other side of the fuselage, engines, and tail design.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the trailing edges of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with small upward-angled winglets at the tips.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows.
